content:
Hello,

I was looking into the state of the Rust USB implementation, and found that what is there is pretty bare. Being completely new to the Linux kernel code, I was interested in tinkering around with adding some abstractions for this. In particular, I was looking at creating an abstraction for struct urb and usb_submit_urb() to submit urbs. What I am finding most difficult to work with are the fields

struct urb {
    ...
    void *context;
    usb_complete_t complete;
    ...
}

I have not come across any existing Rust code in the Linux kernel that handles a situation similar to this, so I was wondering if other more experience developers have any good ideas on how to handle this. A closure seems desirable, since it is like a callback function with its captured environment, but this idea of course doesn't fit with the C structure.

Perhaps someone else is already working on some of these abstractions? I did not come across anything like it on the mailing list.
